title: Race-compound baseline pressure
summary: Normalize tyre age and degradation against causal race-compound context rather than only global compound constants.
rationale: A SOFT tyre can be old at one circuit and normal at another. Race-compound context is more specific than global compound expected life, but it must be computed without peeking at later laps in the same driver sequence.
implementation_hint: Within each race and compound, use current-lap peer context plus optional prior-lap race-compound expanding means/counts for `TyreLife`, `Cumulative_Degradation`, and `LapTime_Delta`. Smooth sparse groups toward global compound constants and add current-minus-baseline features.
expected_effect: Small ROC AUC gain with medium confidence.
risk: Expanding race-compound aggregates are more complex than global medians; ensure temporary sorting does not change returned row order.
